meaning: n.(声音的)抑扬顿挫,节奏,韵律,调子
examples: The music was secondary, if you will, to the dramatic cadence of language, to the way the rhythm of language was used to express feeling and used to add drama.
The French said opera ought to reflect the rhythm and cadence of dramatic literature, bearing in mind that we are talking about the golden age in French literature.
Cadence means the rise and fall of the voice in reading with rhythm.
But boosting that cadence during the middle of a meet is difficult, Mann said.
Jim Miller is executive vice president for products and technologies organization at Cadence Design Systems.
